title: The Peace Maker
content:
آ آ آ آ It has a ï؟½pointï؟½ of neither sex
آ آ آ آ But comes in guise of both,
آ آ آ آ And, doubly dangerous complex,
آ آ آ آ It is a thing to loathe,
آ آ آ آ A lady with her sweet, sad smile,
آ آ آ آ A gentleman on oath.

آ آ آ آ Strip off the mother-veil, and fur!
آ آ آ آ And signs of ï؟½quiet tasteï؟½.
آ آ آ آ The dead childï؟½s locket take from her
آ آ آ آ (The dead manï؟½s gift in haste)
آ آ آ آ And wash from every evil line
آ آ آ آ The layers of filling paste!

آ آ آ آ From ï؟½saddened eyesï؟½ the hellï؟½s own glare!
آ آ آ آ From ï؟½sweet mouthï؟½ blasphemy!
آ آ آ آ Wrench out the gold-filled false teeth there
آ آ آ آ That twice mock honesty,
آ آ آ آ And leave the evil face awry
آ آ آ آ For married folk to see.

آ آ آ آ For foolish girl wives in despair,
آ آ آ آ For menï؟½s and childrenï؟½s sakes,
آ آ آ آ Let loose the glossed and padded hair
آ آ آ آ To writhe like scorching snakes!
آ آ آ آ And strip the barren body bare
آ آ آ آ To show what Satan makes.

آ آ آ آ Aye! I could take her by the throat
آ آ آ آ More sure than hangmanï؟½s noose,
آ آ آ آ And set my teeth and set my nails,
آ آ آ آ And hate would set my thews.
آ آ آ آ And fling her to the drought-starved swine,
آ آ آ آ Were all my brethren Jews.

آ آ آ آ There was the kindest man I knew,
آ آ آ آ Brave, handsome, straight and tall,
آ آ آ آ Between his loved ones and the world
آ آ آ آ He stood, a fortress wall.
آ آ آ آ He whines, a ruined drunkard now,
آ آ آ آ And this thing did it all.

آ آ آ آ There was the girl who married me
آ آ آ آ And bore my children twain,
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ll never meet each otherï؟½s eyes
آ آ آ آ Like boy and girl again.
آ آ آ آ The very childrenï؟½s love and trust
آ آ آ آ By this foul thing was slain.

آ آ آ آ There was a girl my manhood loved,
آ آ آ آ Sheï؟½d Loveï؟½s own red gold hair,
آ آ آ آ And grey eyes that were Pityï؟½s own
آ آ آ آ And courage that was rare.
آ آ آ آ She sleeps amongst the suicides,
آ آ آ آ And this thing sent her there.

آ آ آ آ And all because the town was dull
آ آ آ آ And goodness was too tame,
آ آ آ آ And people took no interest
آ آ آ آ In one they could not blame.
آ آ آ آ And all because my life was clean
آ آ آ آ And I had won a name.

آ آ آ آ And now, for years of senseless hate
آ آ آ آ And paltry, bitter strife,
آ آ آ آ For ï؟½reparationï؟½ come too late,
آ آ آ آ For sweetheart, mate and wife,
آ آ آ آ I tread her vile heart in the dust
آ آ آ آ And ashes of my life.
meaning:
Henry Lawson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Peace Maker"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
